title: Asplundia rivularis
taxonomy: Asplundia rivularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, commonly known as the spiderwort family. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. As a member of the genus Asplundia, it is closely related to other species in the same genus, which are also known for their showy flowers. The classification of Asplundia rivularis is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its long, slender stems and its ability to thrive in shaded environments. This species is often studied by botanists due to its interesting adaptations and its role in the local ecosystems.

intro: Asplundia rivularis, also known as the "climbing orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the family Aspladiaceae.
